page_content:
Program Learning Outcomes

1. Students will demonstrate their ability to ﬁnd and to analyze primary

and secondary sources.

2. Students will be able to think in complex ways about the bias of

sources and to evaluate their reliability and origins.

3. Students will demonstrate their ability to properly cite sources and to understand the range of possibilities of where to search for evidence.

4. Students will show awareness about strategies for testing for invalid

and fabricated information about the past.

5. Students will be able to situate their ﬁndings with regard to earlier

scholarship and to identify their contribution.

6. Students will be able to communicate their ﬁndings clearly and
